title: Zaluzianskya selaginoides
taxonomy: Zaluzianskya selaginoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It belongs to the genus Zaluzianskya and is further classified as a member of the order Lamiales. This plant is native to South Africa and has been assigned the scientific name Zaluzianskya selaginoides. The classification of Zaluzianskya selaginoides is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics. The classification system used is the binomial nomenclature system developed by Carl Linnaeus.

intro: Zaluzianskya selaginoides, commonly known as the evening-scented candytuft or night-scented candytuft, is a flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its sedative and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Z. selaginoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
